response: Upstairs the publisher plans a book with puzzles. Each section needs a unique number of puzzles. The first section holds twelve puzzles. The second section doubles the first section's puzzles. The third section adds five more than the second. The second section has twenty-four puzzles. The third section has twenty-nine puzzles. The total number of puzzles is sixty-five. The editor completes the task successfully. The book contains sixty-five puzzles. Any other questions? * * * The first section has twelve puzzles. The second section has twenty-four puzzles. The third section has twenty-nine puzzles. The total number of puzzles adds up. The sum of all sections is sixty-five. The book is complete. Any other questions? * * * The puzzles are divided into three sections. Each section has a different number of puzzles. The total count is sixty-five puzzles. The editor finishes the task. The book is ready for publication. Any other questions?
